title: Changeable water filter in combination with a mixing valve for pretreatment of water in a home appliance and method of pretreating water
abstract: A water pretreatment system (50, 50', 50") connected to a water supply pipe (P) for supplying water that has been preheated to a home appliance (1, W, 0), including: a mixing valve (52) having an inlet (53) connected to the water supply pipe (P), and a pair of outlets (54, 55); a water filter (56) having an inlet (57) communicated with one of the pair of outlets (54) of the mixing valve (52), and having an outlet (58) communicated with the home appliance (1, W, 0); a bypass line (60) having an inlet end (61) connected to the other of the pair of outlets (55) of the mixing valve (52), and having an outlet end (64) connected to the home appliance (1, W, 0), the mixing valve (52) being configured to at least one of direct water to the water filter (56) or direct water to the bypass line (60); and a controller (66) configured to control the mixing valve (52) such that preheated water supplied to the home appliance (1, W, 0) is one of unfiltered, mixed, or fully filtered by the water filter (56).
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. A water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) connected to a water supply pipe (P) for supplying water that has been preheated to a home appliance (1 , W, O), comprising: a mixing valve (52) having an inlet (53) connected to the water supply pipe (P), and a pair of outlets (54, 55); a water filter (56) having an inlet (57) communicated with one of the pair of outlets (54) of the mixing valve (52), and having an outlet (58) communicated with the home appliance (1 , W, O); a bypass line (60) having an inlet end (61) connected to the other of the pair of outlets (55) of the mixing valve (52), and having an outlet end (64) connected to the home appliance (1 , W, O), the mixing valve (52) being configured to at least one of direct water to the water filter (56) or direct water to the bypass line (60); and a controller (66) configured to control the mixing valve (52) such that preheated water supplied to the home appliance (1 , W, O) is one of unfiltered, mixed, or fully filtered by the water filter (56). 2. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) of claim 1 , wherein the home appliance (1 , W, O) comprises at least one of a dishwasher (1 ), a washing machine (W), or a steam oven (O). 3. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) of claim 1 , wherein the controller (66) comprises a microcontroller. 4. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) of claim 1 , wherein water filter (56) comprises a changeable water filter which has replaceable filter cartridges. 5. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) to one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the preheated water is preheated to approximately 1 10Â°F to 140Â°F. 6. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) to one of claims 1 , 3, 4 or 5, wherein the home appliance (1 , W, O) comprises one of a dishwasher (1 ) or a washing machine (W), and wherein during a first filling of a wash container (2) of the dishwasher (1 ) or washing machine (W), the wash container (2) is filled with unfiltered heated water by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to the bypass line (60) so as to bypass the water filter (56), during a second filling the wash container (2) is filled with both unfiltered water and filtered water for a mixture of both by controlling the mixing valve (52) with the controller (66) to first direct the heated water to the bypass line (60) for a preset time and then after the preset time has elapsed, the controller (66) controls the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water through the water filter (56) for a remainder of the second filling, and during a last 

 filling the wash container (2) is filled with fully filtered heated water by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to only the water filter (56). 7. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) to one of claims 1 , 3, 4 or 5 wherein the home appliance (1 , W, O) comprises a dishwasher (1 ) including a pre wash cycle, a washing cycle, and a rinse cycle, and wherein during a first filling of a wash container (2) of the dishwasher (1 ), the wash container (2) is filled with unfiltered heated water in the pre-wash cycle by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to the bypass line (60) so as to bypass the water filter (56), during a second filling the wash container (2) is filled with both unfiltered water and filtered water for a mixture of both in the washing cycle by controlling the mixing valve (52) with the controller (66) to first direct the hot water to the bypass line (60) for a preset time and to the wash container (2) and then after the preset time has elapsed, the controller (66) controls the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water through the water filter (56) for a remainder of the second filling, and during a last filling the wash container (2) is filled with fully filtered heated water in the rinse cycle by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to only the water filter (56). 8. The water pretreatment system (50, 50â, 50â) to one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the preheated water is directed by the mixing valve (52) to the water filter (56) only during certain cycles of the home appliance (1 , W, O), and the preheated water is 

 directed by the mixing valve (52) to the bypass line (60) so as to bypass the water filter (56) at other times. 9. A method of pretreating water that has been preheated and is supplied to a home appliance (1 , W, 0), the method comprising: filling the home appliance (1 , W, 0) during a first filling with unfiltered heated water by controlling a mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to a bypass line (60) so as to bypass a water filter (56); filling the home appliance (1 , W, 0) during a second filling with both unfiltered water and filtered water for a mixture of both by controlling the mixing valve (52) to first direct the heated water to the bypass line (60) for a preset time and then after the preset time has elapsed, controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water through the water filter (56) for a remainder of the second filling; and filling the home appliance (1 , W, 0) during a last filling with fully filtered heated water by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to only the water filter (56). 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the home appliance (1 , W, 0) comprises at least one of a dishwasher (1 ) or a washing machine (W). 1 1. The method to of claims 9 or 10, wherein the preheated water is preheated to approximately 1 10Â°F to 140Â°F. 12. A method of pretreating water that has been preheated and is supplied to a home appliance (1 , W, 0), the method comprising: supplying the home appliance (1 , W, 0) with unfiltered heated water by controlling a mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to a bypass line (60) so as to bypass a water filter (56); supplying the home appliance with both unfiltered water and filtered water for a mixture of both by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to the bypass line (60) and by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water through the water filter (56); and supplying the home appliance (1 , W, 0) with fully filtered heated water by controlling the mixing valve (52) to direct the heated water to only the water filter (56).
